text.title: File Size Guidelines and AI-Agent Gotchas
text.summary: Enforced size budgets per Laravel file type and common LLM mistakes that collapse the decomposition pattern.

section.title: File size budgets
section.reference: Structural lint script enforcing these budgets in CI

antipattern.description: Pattern collapse: agent adds business logic directly to the controller when asked to "just add an endpoint quickly".
antipattern.reason: Lock the convention in CONTRIBUTING.md and reference it in every system prompt. Without enforcement, agents regress to fat controllers within one sprint.

antipattern.description: DTO drift: same field added to CreateUserDTO and User::$fillable with different names (first_name vs firstName).
antipattern.reason: Enforce snake_case in DB, camelCase only in DTOs with an explicit mapper layer. Two agents writing parallel fields cause silent data loss.

antipattern.description: Resource leaks fields via $this->all() or spread operators.
antipattern.reason: Always explicit field lists in Resources; all() exposes password_hash, remember_token, soft-delete columns.

antipattern.description: Action executing void — def execute(): void.
antipattern.reason: Downstream queue jobs and console commands cannot compose void Actions. Always return the canonical entity or result object.

antipattern.description: Agents invent new layers: "Manager", "Coordinator", "Handler" without an ADR.
antipattern.reason: Whitelist allowed namespaces (Actions, Services, Repositories, DTOs, Policies) and block PRs introducing new ones.
